name: Superman Curse
description: The "Superman Curse" is a persistent, albeit largely tongue-in-cheek, conspiracy theory suggesting that actors and others involved with the Superman franchise are prone to tragic accidents, illnesses, or career setbacks. Proponents claim this is more than just coincidence, citing a pattern of misfortune afflicting those associated with the character across various media adaptations. The alleged curse is said to stem from the inherent hubris of portraying a figure with godlike powers, or alternatively, from some undefined malevolent force attached to the Superman mythos itself. Evidence cited often includes the early deaths of George Reeves (the original TV Superman) and Christopher Reeve (who played Superman in the late 1970s and 1980s), along with various career downturns and personal struggles experienced by other actors, writers, and directors linked to Superman projects. Mainstream perspectives dismiss the "curse" as a case of confirmation bias and selective reporting. With a large number of people involved in Superman productions over decades, statistically, some will inevitably experience hardships. Correlation does not equal causation. Skeptics argue that attributing these events to a supernatural curse ignores the complex and often random nature of life, loss, and the entertainment industry. They point out that many individuals involved with Superman have enjoyed successful and fulfilling lives, further weakening the curse narrative.
subtitle: Alleged misfortunes afflicting Superman franchise participants.

claims.claim: Actors portraying Superman face premature death or severe accidents.
claims.evidence: George Reeves' death, Christopher Reeve's paralysis
claims.criticism: Correlation does not equal causation; statistical probability with a large sample size.

claims.claim: Involvement with Superman projects leads to career decline.
claims.evidence: Examples of actors whose careers stalled after playing Superman roles
claims.criticism: Many factors influence career trajectories; generalizing from a few cases is flawed.

claims.claim: The Superman mythos carries a negative energy or attracts misfortune.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal evidence of various troubles experienced by people involved in Superman projects
claims.criticism: Lacks scientific basis; relies on subjective interpretation and selective reporting.

keyFigures.name: George Reeves
keyFigures.role: Portrayed Superman in the 1950s TV series; his death fueled early curse narratives.

keyFigures.name: Christopher Reeve
keyFigures.role: Played Superman in film franchise; his paralysis after an accident is frequently cited.

keyFigures.name: Snopes.com
keyFigures.role: Debunking the Superman Curse as urban legend and confirmation bias.

timeline.event: George Reeves dies of a gunshot wound, fueling early speculation.
Superman film starring Christopher Reeve is released to great success.
Christopher Reeve is paralyzed in a horse-riding accident.
Christopher Reeve dies, further solidifying the "curse" narrative.
